a.question: Does BDMAS introduction, dramatically lower the activation free energy?
Why is silane half-reaction  without a catalyst at low temperature very difficult?
a.answer: The activation free energies of the two Si–O formation processes amount to 57.8 and 34.3 kcal/mol, respectively, which show that the silane half-reaction between the SiH4 and surface –OH groups without a catalyst at low temperature to be very difficult.

a.question: Which reaction is referred as self-catalysis?
a.answer: By comparison of the activation free energies of the SiH4 and BDMAS half-reactions, it was found that the amino group of the aminosilane can remarkably catalyze the Si–O formation to generate SiO2. We refer to this catalytic aminosilane reaction involving the amino ligand as the self-catalysis

a.question: What can be said about the gibbs free energy profiles of TS1SiH4, P1SiH4, TS2SiH4, TS1A, P1A, and TS2A?
a.answer:
The formation of one Si–O bond requires one amino group and one –OH group. Because the bis-aminosilane precursor has two amino ligands and the
stoichiometric ratio with respect to SiO2 is 1 : 2, BDMAS can produce highly pure and stoichiometric SiO2 films
